texto: Virginia: Oh, you made it. Tony: I was going to say the same to you. I've been here quite a whileVirginia: Really?Tony: Yes, about an hour.Virginia: I didn't know it was a race.Tony: I didn't know that was a path.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kathryn Wesley que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Virginia: Oh, you made it. Tony: I was going to say the same to you. I've been here quite a while…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, path, race, com foco em race e the-10th-kingdom.

frase_original: Virginia: Oh, you made it. Tony: I was going to say the same to you. I've been here quite a whileVirginia: Really?Tony: Yes, about an hour.Virginia: I didn't know it was a race.Tony: I didn't know that was a path.

texto: I thought I had found my true vocation and happiness, but in a strange way, they were just like my stepmother because they didn't want me to grow up either. This is really important you understand, Virginia, because I had gone from something very bad to something very good, but it was only halfway right. They loved me, but they wanted me to stay small like them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kathryn Wesley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I thought I had found my true vocation and happiness, but in a strange way, they were just like m…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, growing-up, snow-white pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de snow-white e the-10th-kingdom.

frase_original: I thought I had found my true vocation and happiness, but in a strange way, they were just like my stepmother because they didn't want me to grow up either. This is really important you understand, Virginia, because I had gone from something very bad to something very good, but it was only halfway right. They loved me, but they wanted me to stay small like them.
